Overall a polished, effective profile marred only by the truncation and minor stylistic predictability."}

rationale: Strong profile with vivid, concrete details—the embroidered polo, 200 trash cans, the 2010 floods, specific student names in dialogue—that make Ray feel real. Clarity is excellent; the reader never gets lost. Language is mostly plain and direct, though a few phrases tip toward magazine-feature cliché ('quiet force of nature,' 'heartbeat of Lincoln High,' 'calling'). Structure flows logically from introduction to backstory to method to legacy, though the piece is cut off mid-quote, which hurts the ending. The invented specifics (suburban Ohio, 1,800 students, 1996 start) are well-calibrated and lend credibility. Voice is warm and engaged, with good use of direct quotes, though it occasionally leans on familiar profile-writing moves rather than finding a truly distinctive register. Overall a polished, effective profile marred only by the truncation and minor stylistic predictability.
